facts.statement: ConocoPhillips is one of the world's leading exploration and production companies, based on both production and reserves, with a globally diversified asset portfolio, headquartered in Houston, Texas.
ConocoPhillips reported revenue of $48.522 billion for FY2025.
ConocoPhillips reported net income of $10.957 billion for FY2025.
ConocoPhillips generated $19.965 billion in operating cash flow for FY2025.
As of December 31, 2025, ConocoPhillips had stockholders' equity of $48.003 billion and cash and equivalents of $5.607 billion.
ConocoPhillips' production averaged 2,375 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day (MBOED) in 2025.
As of December 31, 2025, ConocoPhillips had proved reserves of 7.6 billion barrels of oil equivalent.
As of December 31, 2025, ConocoPhillips had operations and activities in 14 countries, $122 billion of total assets, and approximately 9,900 employees.
In 2025, ConocoPhillips achieved its target of returning 45% of cash from operations (CFO) to shareholders through buybacks and the dividend.
In December 2025, ConocoPhillips increased its ordinary dividend by 8%, in line with its commitment to grow the base dividend at a top-quartile S&P 500 rate.

ConocoPhillips successfully completed the integration of Marathon Oil Corporation, capturing more than $1 billion of synergies and approximately $1 billion of additional one-time benefits, while identifying an incremental $1 billion in cost reduction and margin enhancement opportunities to be captured on a run-rate basis by year-end 2026.

ConocoPhillips advanced the Willow project in Alaska and equity LNG projects at North Field East and North Field South in Qatar and Port Arthur LNG (PALNG) on the U.S. Gulf Coast, and secured total commercial LNG offtake of 10 MTPA.
ConocoPhillips achieved a more than 15% improvement in Lower 48 drilling and completion efficiencies year over year in 2025.

In 2025, ConocoPhillips completed approved emissions abatement projects and met its annual greenhouse gas emissions intensity target, advancing its goal to reduce emissions intensity by 50 to 60% by 2030, and reached zero routine flaring for heritage ConocoPhillips assets.
Ryan M. Lance serves as Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer of ConocoPhillips.
Andrew M. O'Brien serves as Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President, Strategy and Commercial (principal financial officer) of ConocoPhillips.
Kontessa S. Haynes-Welsh serves as Vice President, Finance and Controller (principal accounting officer) of ConocoPhillips.

Robert A. Niblock serves as Lead Director of the ConocoPhillips Board, and the Board has recommended a vote against a stockholder proposal for an Independent Board Chairman at the 2026 Annual Meeting.

ConocoPhillips' Board of Directors consists of 13 director nominees standing for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting, including directors Dennis V. Arriola, Nelda J. Connors, Gay Huey Evans, Jeffrey A. Joerres, Ryan M. Lance, Timothy A. Leach, Kathleen A. McGinty, William H. McRaven, Sharmila Mulligan, Arjun N. Murti, Robert A. Niblock, David T. Seaton, and R.A. Walker.

In 2025, ConocoPhillips incurred total costs of $12,364 million in consolidated operations for oil and gas activities, including $11,174 million in development costs and $928 million in exploration costs.

The standardized measure of discounted future net cash flows relating to proved oil and gas reserve quantities was $55,962 million for total company (consolidated operations and equity affiliates) at year-end 2025, down from $70,863 million at year-end 2024.

During 2025, ConocoPhillips classified certain noncore Lower 48 assets, including Ursa/Europa Fields and the Ursa Oil Pipeline Company LLC, and Lower 48 assets in the Anadarko Basin, as disposal groups disposed of by sale.
ConocoPhillips reported research and development expense of $81 million for FY2025.
ConocoPhillips is classified under the SEC SIC description of 'Petroleum Refining' and is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol COP.
